lede: A look at July 15 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Iain Armitage and Neemia Tialata, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: France national football team
events.desc: France win their second World Cup title, defeating Croatia 4–2.

events.title: Psy
events.desc: South Korean rapper Psy releases his hit single Gangnam Style.

events.title: Caspian Airlines Flight 7908
events.desc: Caspian Airlines Flight 7908 crashes near Jannatabad, Qazvin, Iran, killing 168.

events.title: Taliban
events.desc: "American Taliban" John Walker Lindh pleads guilty to supplying aid to the enemy and possession of explosives during the commission of a felony.

events.title: Space Race
events.desc: Space Race: Apollo–Soyuz Test Project features the dual launch of an Apollo spacecraft and a Soyuz spacecraft on the first Soviet-United States human-crewed flight. It was the last launch of both an Apollo spacecraft, and the Saturn family of rockets.

events.title: Boeing 367-80
events.desc: The Boeing 367-80, the prototype for both the Boeing 707 and C-135 series, takes its first flight.

events.title: Seattle
events.desc: In Seattle, Washington, William Boeing and George Conrad Westervelt incorporate Pacific Aero Products (later renamed Boeing).

events.title: Ralph Waldo Emerson
events.desc: Ralph Waldo Emerson delivers the Divinity School Address at Harvard Divinity School, discounting Biblical miracles and declaring Jesus a great man, but not God. The Protestant community reacts with outrage.

events.title: French Revolution
events.desc: French Revolution: Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de Lafayette, is named by acclamation Colonel General of the new National Guard of Paris.

events.title: Swedish–Novgorodian Wars
events.desc: Swedish–Novgorodian Wars: A Novgorodian army led by Alexander Nevsky defeats the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva.

births.name: Jocelyn Bell Burnell
births.line: Northern Irish astrophysicist, astronomer, and academic

deaths.text: Peter R. de Vries, Dutch investigative journalist and crime reporter
Leopold Auer, Hungarian violinist, composer, and conductor
James Scott, Duke of Monmouth, 1st Duke of Monmouth, Dutch-born English general and claimant to the throne, executed

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bernhard II, Margrave of Baden-Baden
Christian feast day: Dispersion of the Apostles (formerly by the Catholic Church)
